Home Curriculum Student Work About Support Become a Partner

Building the future of Zimbabwe, one student at a time.

By 2026: 30 Zimbabweans employed. 2,000 learning.

Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe

The MCRI is a post-secondary institution located in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe. Students across Zimbabwe who have attended one of our MATTER Innovation Hub partner schools are able to apply by recommendation from their facilitator.

Feeder Schools

  • Mosi-oa-Tonya
  • Jafuta Center
  • King George VI
  • Dominican Convent
  • Gateway Senior High School
  • Nechilibi High School

Goal

By 2026, 30 Zimbabweans employed & 2,000 learning

Strategy

(1) Kinder-to-career (2) Software engineering (3) Partnerships

Mission

Empower students in Zimbabwe to achieve career success

Purpose

To help Zimbabwe achieve a thriving, self-sufficient economy

Belief

Power of education & employment to transform lives & economies

We take care of our students.

We created a place where we would want to send our own loved ones.

  • Food & board provided
  • Health insurance
  • Monthly stipend
  • Housing across the street

At least 50% girls and young women.

In order to create change, it starts early by teaching boys and young men how to respect women. By being vigilant in including women in these spaces, we break down barriers and show that women belong in all places that men do.

Built to Last

Solar Powered

All our buildings run 100% on solar energy.

Local Construction

All construction and outfitting is hired and sourced locally, supporting the community economy.

Universal Design

All structures are built with universal design in mind, so every student participates like their peers.

Global Impact Map

28

Hubs

8

Countries

Meet the Team

Kelly Watkins Conrad

Senior Program Engineer, Jamf CEI

Kelly leads curriculum design and technical instruction at the MCRI. With deep expertise in Apple platforms and enterprise device management, she translates Jamf's real-world workforce requirements into a curriculum that produces graduates who are ready to work from day one. Kelly travels to Victoria Falls each cohort to deliver instruction in person alongside the MCRI team.

LinkedIn

Valeria Tschida

Senior Program Manager, Jamf CEI

Valeria manages program operations, student support, and the logistics that keep the MCRI running cohort after cohort. She developed the TEAL+ instructional model that forms the foundation of every student's learning journey — before they ever walk through the MCRI doors. Valeria ensures every student has what they need to succeed, from curriculum resources to daily wellbeing.

LinkedIn

David J. Saltmarsh

Senior Director, Community Education Initiatives, Jamf

Dave founded and leads Jamf's Community Education Initiatives, which powers the MCRI and a global network of 28 MATTER Innovation Hubs across 8 countries. He built the employer partnerships that give MCRI graduates a direct path to full-time remote work, and he champions the program's commitment to gender equity and universal design. Dave is the person to contact if you want to get involved.

LinkedIn

Jamf CEI Mission

The Jamf Community Education Initiatives team lives Jamf's values of selflessness and relentless self-improvement out in the world, where we enable students to think creatively to solve problems, learn the value of being better together, and how to empathize with others. Our goal is not to teach technology but to use it as a tool to help people become the best version of themselves.

Learn More

The TEAL+ Model

Technology-Enabled Active Learning — the pedagogical foundation that prepares students before they arrive at the MCRI.

Learn about TEAL+

Kinder-to-Career

The MCRI is the final stop in a continuum that begins in primary school. Understand the full journey.

Explore Kinder-to-Career

Join us in building something remarkable.

Ways to Support the MCRI

A post-secondary technology institution in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe — with a direct pathway to full-time employment.
